title: The New Caledonia Congress Has a New, Pro-France President. What Now?
author: Catherine Wilson
text:
Virginie Ruffenach (2nd from right), president of the New Caledonia Congress, and French lawmaker Nicolas Metzdorf (2nd from left) attend French National Day (Bastille Day) celebrations in Noumea, New Caledonia, July 14, 2026.
Credit: Congress of New Caledonia
The French overseas territory of New Caledonia in the southwest Pacific, which has endured a decade of political turbulence, social tensions, and economic shocks, has emerged from elections held on June 28. Virginie Ruffenach, leader of the French loyalist Rassemblement (The Rally) Party, was elected president of the New Caledonia Congress on July 10 after negotiating a coalition with the Polynesian Islander-led Eveil Océanien (Oceanian Awakening) Party to achieve the majority to govern. Ruffenach emphasized unity and dialogue in her inaugural speech as she prepares to preside over a Congress divided between the pro-independence and loyalist blocs.[...]
